recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the history of the Zhou Dynasty, especially the transition from the Western Zhou to the Eastern Zhou period. The fact that the Zhou Dynasty was the last completely feudal dynasty in Chinese history and its impact on the subsequent Qin Dynasty is intriguing. I'm also curious about the cultural and social developments during this period, such as the formation of the Confucian classics and the emergence of regional cultures. The mention of the "Mandate of Heaven" and the role of the Zhou kings as the highest authority also catches my attention. I'd like to learn more about the specific events and figures that shaped this dynasty.
